title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: init ordering dependency undocumented
description:
Cross-file interaction issue: lichen_l2_iface_init() depends on lichen_lora_l2_init() being called first.

lichen_l2.c:1473 calls lichen_lora_l2_init()
lichen_l2.c:1487 calls lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() which returns NULL if not initialized

The dependency chain is:
1. NET_DEVICE_INIT runs lichen_l2_iface_init
2. Which calls lichen_lora_l2_init (lora_l2.c:546)
3. Which generates EUI-64 and transitions UNINIT->STOPPED
4. Then lichen_l2.c can call get_eui64()

This ordering is currently enforced by lichen_l2_iface_init calling init() first. But:

FRAGILITY: If someone calls lichen_lora_l2_start() from main() before NET_DEVICE_INIT runs (wrong init priority), start() will fail with -EINVAL because state==UNINIT.

The init priority (CONFIG_KERNEL_INIT_PRIORITY_DEFAULT) should be documented as depending on lora0 device being ready. Comment at line 1389-1395 mentions this but is vague about failure mode.

FIX: Add BUILD_ASSERT or runtime check that lichen_lora_l2_init is called before any lichen_l2 operation, with clear error message.
close_reason: Documented and enforced LoRa L2 init ordering contract; verified with fresh LoRa-enabled T-Echo bridge build
